title: Messaging Outcomes and Delivery Evidence in Go · Runtime Contract: Transfer Evidence Plan
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must separate protocol custody from application effects with explicit structs and table-driven outcomes; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the transfer evidence plan increment in a typed outcome and ownership evidence record. Learners progress through predictio…
objectives: Plan browser models, Go compilation, vet, table tests, fuzzing, race and leak checks, disposable RabbitMQ integration, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production transfer gates.

Map message ownership from producer intent through local buffer, broker confirmation, queue, delivery, consumer acknowledgement, and durable effect; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Transfer Evidence Plan · Theory Transfer Evidence Plan
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 5670 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate local publication, broker custody, and observable stakeholder effect; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement owner; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan browser models, Go compilation, vet, table tests, fuzzing, race and leak checks, disposable RabbitMQ integration, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production transfer gates.
